question.text: An unlisted intermediary has 5 shareholders holding 25%, 20%, 18%, 15%, and 22% voting rights respectively. How many of these shareholders are required to meet the 'fit and proper person' criteria under the voting rights threshold?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** As per the Fit and Proper Criteria, in case of an unlisted applicant or intermediary, any person holding twenty percent or more voting rights is required to fulfil the criteria. Three shareholders hold 20% or more: 25%, 20%, and 22%. **The Trap:** Candidates may forget that the threshold is 'twenty percent or more' (inclusive of 20%) and exclude the shareholder holding exactly 20%. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When assessing fit and proper requirements, always check the exact threshold - 'or more' means the boundary value is included.
